findings.description: CSV/formula injection (CWE-1236): export() writes attacker-controlled name/email fields verbatim with no neutralization of leading =,+,-,@,TAB,CR, so the 'cleaned' CSV opened in Excel/Sheets/LibreOffice executes formulas (=WEBSERVICE/=HYPERLINK exfil, DDE =cmd|'/c calc'!A0 command exec).

Unvalidated path passed straight to open() in count_rows() is an arbitrary-file-read / existence-and-size oracle (CWE-22/CWE-73); readlines() also loads the whole file into memory (CWE-400 DoS) and the file handle is never closed.

parse() hardcodes row[0..3] (the computed header 'index' on line 36 is dead code) and wraps int()/float()/indexing with no error handling, so a short row (IndexError) or non-numeric field (ValueError) raises an unhandled exception — input-driven crash/DoS (CWE-20).

parse() calls list(reader) to fully materialize untrusted input with no row-count/size cap (CWE-400 memory-exhaustion DoS), and stream_valid() routes through it so its advertised 'streaming' property is defeated and offers no protection against oversized uploads.
float(row[2]) accepts 'nan'/'inf'/'-inf' from attacker input (CWE-20 incomplete validation), poisoning summarize() so total/avg become NaN/Inf and corrupting any downstream aggregation or comparison.

export() relies on os.path.basename() which blocks directory traversal but provides no extension allowlist or overwrite protection, so an attacker-chosen filename can clobber arbitrary existing files within export_dir.

Constructor calls os.makedirs(export_dir, exist_ok=True) with no mode argument and no caller validation of export_dir, creating directories with default (potentially world-readable) permissions for data described as cleaned/exportable (CWE-732/CWE-276).
